title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Automation and Data Client Systems · Independent Program Trial
summary: A research archive team integrating an unfamiliar public dataset must satisfy changed endpoint, payload, authority, byte, and time constraints without a recipe for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers spec-aware cache decision notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Private Shared Cache
steps.instruction:
lab case — research archive client stores a response with max-age=40, ETag "rev-4", Age:4, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

What must be established before implementation?

At Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Stale Revalidation Invalidation, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before running it: research archive client stores a response with max-age=20, ETag "rev-2", Age:2, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

For Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Stale Revalidation Invalidation, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Deleting a local entry guarantees every intermediary stops serving stale content. This remains false for Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers.

content.title: Evidence defense
